title: Sesquicella macrorhiza
intro: Sesquicella macrorhiza, commonly known as Wild Yam or Dioscorea, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Dioscoreaceae family, native to North America. The health benefits of Wild Yam include its potential in alleviating menopausal symptoms, such as hot flashes and mood swings, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help in reducing pain and improving overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Wild Yam include diosgenin, a plant-based steroid saponin that has been shown to exhibit estrogenic activity, as well as other compounds like dioscorin and dioscorine. Wild Yam is commonly prepared in herbal supplements, teas, and tinctures, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat a range of health conditions, including menstrual cramps and rheumatoid arthritis.
properties: Sesquicella macrorhiza is reported to possess various therapeutic properties, although the exact details are limited in my knowledge.
